title: asparagales
intro: Asparagales is an order of flowering plants, characterized by their unique floral structure and typically consisting of a single whorl of three petals and three sepals. This order includes a diverse range of families, such as Orchidaceae, which comprises orchids, Euphorbiaceae, a family of plants that includes the spurge and poinsettia, and Cyperaceae, a family of grass-like plants commonly known as sedges. Many plants within the Asparagales order are known for their distinctive, often showy flowers and ability to thrive in a variety of environments. The Asparagales order encompasses over 20,000 species, showcasing an incredible range of diversity within this group of flowering plants.

families.family_desc: Orchidaceae is a family of flowering plants commonly known as orchids, characterized by showy, exotic, and highly diverse flowers.
Amaryllidaceae is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the amaryllis or bellflower family, characterized by showy, funnel-shaped flowers and narrow, strap-like leaves.
Asparagaceae is a family of flowering plants that includes asparagus, hyacinths, lilies, and other ornamental and edible species.
Asphodelaceae is a family of flowering plants commonly known as asphodels or asphodel irises, which includes species such as asparagus and lilyturf.
Alstroemeriaceae is a family of flowering plants, commonly known as the glory lily or parrot's beak, native to the Americas.
Hypoxidaceae is a family of flowering plants in the order Asparagales, commonly known as the starflowers or hypoxis family.
Ixioliriaceae is a small family of flowering plants in the order Asterales, commonly known as the snake flowers.
Asteliaceae is a small family of flowering plants in the order Asparagales, native to New Zealand and southern South America.
Blandfordiaceae is a family of epiphytic orchids, commonly known as the Blandfordia or Christmas bells.
Tecophilaeaceae is a small family of flowering plants commonly known as the bloodroot or red-lobe families.
Doryanthaceae is a family of flowering plants, commonly known as the Midge Orchids, native to eastern Australia.
Petermanniaceae is a family of flowering plants in the order Poales, commonly known as grasses or sedges.
